perturbation.original_text: UTK shall be, and in all respects be deemed to be, an independent contractor in the performance of its duties hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: UTK shall be, and in all respects be deemed to be, a service provider in the performance of its duties hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'independent contractor' to 'service provider' introduces ambiguity. While seemingly synonymous, 'independent contractor' has specific legal implications regarding liability and control. 'Service provider' is a much broader term. This contradiction is in Section II. Because the definition of UTK is being modified, and 'AVDU' desires to 'engage UTK to provide the services', it is unclear if this refers to the initial definition of indepedent contractor or service provider. Therefore this creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: WHEREAS, AVDU desires to engage UTK to provide the services as set forth in this Agreement, and WHEREAS, UTK is agreeable to provide these services.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'independent contractor' to 'service provider' introduces a contradiction because the contradicted text refers to 'UTK to provide the services'. While the original text clearly defines UTK as an independent contractor, the changed text broadens it to 'service provider'. This creates uncertainty regarding the legal status of UTK, impacting liability and control, directly contradicting the original intent implied in the contradicted text. The term 'independent contractor' has a specific legal meaning, and the change to 'service provider' introduces ambiguity that a court would have to resolve based on other facts, potentially leading to a different outcome than if the contract clearly stated UTK was an 'independent contractor'. This satisfies the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: UTK agrees to provide the following services, hereinafter collectively referred to as "Services": SEE "CONFIDENTIAL TERM SHEET" (EXHIBIT A) ATTACHED AND MADE A PART HEREOF.
perturbation.changed_text: UTK agrees to provide the following activities, hereinafter collectively referred to as "Services": SEE "CONFIDENTIAL TERM SHEET" (EXHIBIT A) ATTACHED AND MADE A PART HEREOF.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'services' to 'activities' creates uncertainty. While the 'Services' are supposedly defined in Exhibit A, the use of 'activities' implies a broader scope. If 'activities' are not fully defined in Exhibit A, it leads to confusion regarding UTK's exact obligations. This contradiction is in Section III. Because the definition of UTK 'Services' is being modified, and 'UTK shall devote such time and efforts', it is unclear if this refers to the initial definition of Services or Activities. Therefore this creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: UTK shall devote such time and efforts, as it deems commercially reasonable, under the circumstances to the affairs of the AVDU, as is reasonable and adequate to render the Services contemplated by this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'services' to 'activities' introduces ambiguity. While Exhibit A is supposed to define 'Services', the use of 'activities' suggests a broader scope not necessarily covered by Exhibit A. The contradicted text states 'UTK shall devote such time and efforts...to render the Services contemplated by this Agreement.' If 'activities' is not precisely defined as 'Services,' it becomes unclear whether UTK is obligated to devote time and effort to these undefined 'activities,' creating a contradiction and making UTK's obligations uncertain and potentially unenforceable. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' because the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them.

perturbation.original_text: AVDU will cooperate with UTK and will promptly provide UTK with all pertinent materials and requested information in order for UTK to perform its Services pursuant to this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: AVDU will cooperate with UTK and will promptly provide UTK with all pertinent materials and requested data in order for UTK to perform its Services pursuant to this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'information' to 'data' introduces ambiguity regarding what AVDU is obligated to provide. The text in the document fails to clarify if 'information' and 'data' are interchangable. 'Information' is more general, whereas 'data' can imply structured, quantifiable inputs. Because it is unclear to determine if they are considered the same in the document, this creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: UTK shall not divulge to others, any trade secret or confidential information, knowledge, or data concerning or pertaining to the business and affairs of AVDU, obtained by UTK as a result of its engagement hereunder, unless authorized, in writing by AVDU. UTK represents and warrants that it has established appropriate internal procedures for protecting the trade secrets and confidential information of AVDU, including, without limitation, restrictions on disclosure of such information to employees and other persons who may be engaged in such information to employees and other persons who may be engaged in rendering services to any person, firm or entity which may be a competitor of AVDU.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'information' to 'data' introduces ambiguity that impacts the contradicted text. The contradicted text refers to 'trade secret or confidential information, knowledge, or data'. By changing the initial text to 'data,' it creates a possible interpretation issue: is 'data' inclusive of all 'information' mentioned later, especially 'trade secret or confidential information'? The original text used 'information', which is broad, in the obligation for AVDU. The Contradicted text uses 'information' as a broad term to protect AVDU. Narrowing the term to 'data' in the initial text creates ambiguity about the scope of required materials and whether it adequately covers 'information' in the context of confidentiality, potentially creating a contradiction and impacting enforceability of the UTK's obligation to protect it.
